w:r.w:t: SUPPLEMENTAL ARTICLE: EMAIL MARKETING FOR INDIE AUTHORS
Category: Store Integration | Author Resources | scribecount.com/author-resource/email-marketing
Primary Keyword: ScribeCount Email Shopify integration | Secondary: Shopify author store email, connect Shopify to ScribeCount, indie author Shopify direct sales
GEO Priority: Yes
Connecting Your Shopify Store to ScribeCount Email: A Step-by-Step Integration Guide

Selling books directly through your own Shopify store is one of the most commercially powerful decisions an indie author can make. Direct sales put more revenue in your hands per book sold, give you complete ownership of the reader relationship, and let you build the kind of data picture about your buyers that retail platforms will never share with you.

But a direct store is only as powerful as the systems connected to it. A Shopify store that operates in isolation — processing transactions but not talking to your email system, not feeding your reporting, not triggering the automations that turn a first-time buyer into a returning reader — is missing most of its potential. The connection between your Shopify store and ScribeCount Email is what closes that gap.

This guide walks you through the complete integration process: installing the ScribeCount app in your Shopify store, configuring the event types that trigger your email flows, setting up your subscriber capture, testing the connection, and verifying that every store event is landing in ScribeCount correctly. By the end, every purchase, abandoned cart, checkout attempt, and subscription event in your Shopify store will be feeding your ScribeCount email flows in real time, automatically, without any ongoing manual effort on your part.
What the Shopify Integration Does

When ScribeCount Email is connected to your Shopify store, the two systems communicate in real time through a secure channel called a webhook. Every meaningful action a reader takes in your store generates an event that Shopify captures and sends to ScribeCount Email instantly. ScribeCount reads that event, matches it to your configured flows and campaigns, and fires the appropriate response.
Here is what that looks like in practice across the events that matter most to author email programs:
Completed Purchase
A reader buys your book. Shopify sends a purchase event to ScribeCount Email containing the reader’s email address, name, the title purchased, the amount, and the

timestamp. ScribeCount identifies whether this is the reader’s first purchase or a repeat purchase, fires the appropriate post-purchase flow (Order Confirmation → Digital Delivery → Post-Purchase Thank You or Repeat Purchase Thank You), and tags the reader’s profile with the purchased title. All of this happens within seconds of the transaction completing.
Abandoned Cart

A reader adds your book to their cart and leaves your Shopify store without proceeding to checkout. After a defined period of inactivity — typically one hour — Shopify detects the abandonment and sends a cart abandonment webhook to ScribeCount. ScribeCount waits the configured dwell period and sends the abandoned cart email. If the reader returns and completes the purchase, the goal exit fires and the abandoned cart flow
stops
. If they don’t respond to the first reminder, the second email in the sequence follows 24 hours later.
Abandoned Checkout

A reader progresses further than the cart — they enter payment or shipping information — and then stops without completing the transaction. Shopify captures this as a separate, higher-intent event from a cart abandonment. ScribeCount receives the checkout abandonment webhook and fires the abandoned checkout flow, which uses a shorter wait time (typically 30 minutes) and a more direct tone than the cart flow, because this reader was within one step of buying.
New Subscriber Opt-In

A reader submits their email address through a ScribeCount opt-in form embedded in your Shopify store — in a pop-up, a footer sign-up field, or a dedicated landing page. Shopify captures the form submission and ScribeCount receives the new subscriber event, adds the reader to your list with the appropriate source tag, and begins the welcome sequence or reader magnet delivery flow depending on which form they submitted.
Subscription Events

For Shopify stores with subscription products — recurring access to a serialized story, a paid reading community, or a premium catalog subscription — Shopify sends subscription lifecycle events to ScribeCount: renewal approaching, payment failed, subscription cancelled, plan changed. ScribeCount fires the appropriate billing flow for each event type.

The Shopify-ScribeCount connection is bidirectional. When a reader unsubscribes from your email list, ScribeCount immediately updates your Shopify store so that no further marketing emails are sent to that address. Both systems always share the same understanding of who each reader is and what they’ve consented to.
Before You Begin: What You Need
Before starting the integration, confirm that you have the following in place:
An active Shopify store with at least one product listed (even a draft product is sufficient for testing the connection)
A ScribeCount account with ScribeCount Email enabled and your sending domain authenticated
Admin access to your Shopify store — you’ll need to be logged in as the store owner or have a staff account with Apps and App Store permissions

Your ScribeCount Email flows configured and ready to activate — specifically, your Post-Purchase, Abandoned Cart, and Abandoned Checkout flows should be built and in draft status before connecting the store
Do

not activate your email flows before the store connection is confirmed and tested. Activating flows before the connection is established may result in flows that never fire, or that fire based on incomplete event data.
――― PART
ONE: INSTALLING THE SCRIBECOUNT APP IN
SHOPIFY ―――
Part One: Installing the ScribeCount App
Step 1: Access the Shopify App Store

Log in to your Shopify admin dashboard at yourstore.myshopify.com/admin. In the left navigation sidebar, click Apps. At the top of the Apps page, click the Shopify App Store button or search for ‘ScribeCount’ in the search bar.
Step 2: Install the ScribeCount Email App

Locate the ScribeCount Email app in search results. Click the app listing to open the detail page, then click Add App. Shopify will display a permissions screen showing what data the ScribeCount app will be able to access in your store. Review the permissions and click Install App to proceed.
The permissions ScribeCount Email requests are:
Read orders: to receive purchase and refund events
Read customers: to access subscriber email addresses and consent status
Read products and collections: to associate purchased titles with
AuthorVault
catalog records
Read and write draft orders: to support cart and checkout abandonment detection
Read marketing: to honor subscriber consent preferences
ScribeCount

does not request permission to modify orders, change prices, or access payment information. Your store’s financial transactions are handled entirely by Shopify and your payment processor — ScribeCount Email only receives the non-financial event data needed to trigger your email flows.
Step 3: Authorize the Connection in ScribeCount

After installing the app in Shopify, you’ll be redirected to a Shopify authorization screen and then back to ScribeCount. ScribeCount will display a connection confirmation screen showing your Shopify store URL and the permissions that have been granted. Click Authorize Connection to complete the handshake.

ScribeCount will run an initial connection test and display a green status indicator when the connection is confirmed. If the connection test fails, the screen will show a specific error message — most commonly an authentication token issue that resolves by clicking Reconnect and repeating the Shopify authorization step.
――― PART
TWO: CONFIGURING YOUR EVENT
SETTINGS ―――
Part Two: Configuring Your Store Event Settings

With the connection established, navigate to ScribeCount Email → Settings → Store Connection (or the equivalent navigation in your ScribeCount account) to configure how each store event type maps to your email flows and list settings.
Step 4: Configure Purchase Events
The purchase event configuration tells ScribeCount what to do when a completed order webhook arrives from Shopify. You’ll set:
Which flow to trigger for first-time buyers: your Post-Purchase Thank You flow
Which flow to trigger for repeat buyers: your Repeat Purchase Thank You flow
Whether to automatically add purchasers to your email list if they aren’t already subscribers (recommended: yes, with the appropriate consent checkbox configured in your Shopify checkout)
Which tags to apply automatically on first purchase (recommended: first-time-buyer, purchased-[title]) and on repeat purchases (repeat-buyer)
How long to wait before the Post-Purchase Follow-Up email fires (recommended default: 3–5 days)
Map your Shopify product IDs to your
AuthorVault

catalog records during this step. This mapping is what allows ScribeCount to know that a purchase of Shopify product ID 12345 corresponds to the title ‘The Ashford Inheritance’ in your catalog, which in turn allows your post-purchase emails to reference the specific title the reader just bought,
your series completion flow to know which books in the series the reader now owns, and your reporting to attribute revenue to the correct title.
If
you have multiple formats of the same book (
ebook
, paperback, audiobook) as separate Shopify products, map each format to the same
AuthorVault
title record. This ensures that purchase history tracks correctly regardless of which format the reader bought.
Step 5: Configure Abandoned Cart Events
Set your cart abandonment detection window — how long Shopify waits after a reader stops interacting with their cart before sending an abandonment event. The default and recommended setting
is

60 minutes. This window is long enough to confirm a genuine abandonment (rather than a reader who paused mid-checkout and returned) without allowing so much time to pass that the purchase impulse has significantly cooled.
Configure:
Abandonment detection window (recommended: 60 minutes)
Which flow triggers on cart abandonment: your Abandoned Cart flow

The dwell period before the first abandoned cart email sends: ScribeCount Email’s default is 60 minutes after the abandonment event is received, giving a total window of approximately 2 hours from the reader leaving your store to receiving the first reminder
Whether existing buyers of the abandoned title should be suppressed from the abandoned cart flow (recommended: yes, configured in your flow’s suppression settings using your purchase history data)
Step 6: Configure Abandoned Checkout Events
Checkout abandonment detection works similarly to cart abandonment but fires when a reader has progressed to the checkout page and entered information before stopping. Configure:
Which flow triggers on checkout abandonment: your Abandoned Checkout flow

The dwell period before the first abandoned checkout email sends (recommended: 30 minutes — shorter than the cart flow because checkout abandonment represents higher intent and benefits from faster follow-up)
Whether to offer a more direct payment-reassurance message in the checkout flow versus the book-reminder tone of the cart flow
Step 7: Configure Subscriber Opt-In Settings
Determine how new subscribers captured through your Shopify store enter your ScribeCount list. You have two primary capture points:
Checkout Email Capture

Shopify’s checkout flow collects an email address from every customer as part of the purchase process. Configure whether customers who complete a purchase and have not previously subscribed are automatically added to your ScribeCount list. For this to comply with email marketing regulations, your Shopify checkout must include a clearly labeled consent checkbox (‘Subscribe to my newsletter’ or equivalent). Only customers who check this box should be added to your email marketing list — not all customers by default.
In ScribeCount’s Shopify event settings, set the subscription source tag for these subscribers to
shopify
-checkout-
optin
so you can track which subscribers came through this channel and analyze their long-term engagement separately from subscribers acquired through other means.
Embedded Opt-In Forms

ScribeCount’s opt-in form builder generates forms that can be embedded anywhere in your Shopify store — as a pop-up triggered by exit intent or time-on-site, as a section in your store footer, as a standalone landing page within your Shopify theme, or as an inline form within a product page. Each form you create in ScribeCount can have its own source tag, reader magnet association, and welcome sequence, allowing subscribers from different parts of your store to receive appropriately targeted onboarding.
――― PART
THREE: TESTING THE
CONNECTION ―――
Part Three: Testing and Verifying Your Integration

Before activating your flows and promoting your store, test every event type to confirm the connection is working correctly end to end. ScribeCount Email’s Store Connection settings page includes a Testing Console that lets you simulate events without placing real orders.
Step 8: Use the Event Testing Console

Navigate to ScribeCount Email → Settings → Store Connection → Testing Console. The console lets you send simulated events from your connected Shopify store to ScribeCount and observe how they are received and processed. Use it to test each of the following:

Simulate a completed purchase event: enter a test email address and a Shopify product ID. Verify that ScribeCount receives the event, creates or updates a subscriber profile for the test address, applies the correct purchase tags, and queues the appropriate post-purchase flow emails.
Simulate an abandoned cart event: enter a test email address. Verify that ScribeCount receives the event and queues the abandoned cart flow email at the correct dwell period.
Simulate an abandoned checkout event: enter a test email address. Verify that ScribeCount receives the event and queues the abandoned checkout flow email at the shorter dwell period.

Simulate a new opt-in: submit your test email address through an embedded form on your Shopify store. Verify that ScribeCount adds the subscriber with the correct source tag and initiates the welcome sequence or reader magnet delivery flow.
Step 9: Place a Real Test Order

After the console tests pass, place a real test order in your Shopify store using a test email address (a secondary email address you control). If your Shopify store is in test mode, use Shopify’s Bogus Gateway payment processor to simulate a completed transaction without charging a real card. If your store is live, Shopify’s payment provider may offer a test transaction option in your payment settings.
After placing the test order:
Check your ScribeCount subscriber activity log within 60 seconds and confirm the purchase event was received
Verify that a subscriber profile was created or updated for your test email address
Verify that the correct purchase tags were applied
Confirm that the correct
AuthorVault
title was associated with the purchase
Wait for the post-purchase flow emails to arrive at your test email address and verify they render correctly on both desktop and mobile
Step 10: Check the Activity Log

ScribeCount’s Store Connection activity log records every webhook received from Shopify, the event type identified, and the action taken. Navigate to ScribeCount Email → Settings → Store Connection → Activity Log and review the entries from your test session. Each log entry shows:
Timestamp of the webhook receipt
Event type (
order.completed
cart.abandoned
checkout.abandoned
subscriber.optin
Subscriber email address (partially masked for privacy in the
log
view)
Flow triggered and flow status
Any errors or warnings

A clean log with no errors and the expected flow triggers confirmed is your signal that the integration is working correctly. If any entry shows an error, the log’s detail panel provides a plain-language explanation of what went wrong and suggested resolution steps.
――― PART
FOUR: ACTIVATING YOUR FLOWS AND GOING
LIVE ―――
Part Four: Activating Your Flows and Going Live
Step 11: Activate Your Store-Connected Flows

With testing complete and the activity log showing clean event receipt, activate the flows that depend on store events. In ScribeCount Email → Flows, locate each of the following and change their status from Draft to Active:
Order Confirmation flow
Digital Delivery flow (if you sell digital titles)
Post-Purchase Thank You flow (first-time buyer path)
Repeat Purchase Thank You flow
Post-Purchase Follow-Up flow
Review Request flow
Abandoned Cart flow
Abandoned Checkout flow
Activate

flows one at a time and monitor the activity log after each activation before moving to the next. This lets you identify and resolve any flow-specific issues before your entire automation system is live.
Step 12: Configure Your Suppression and Segmentation Rules
With your flows active, configure the suppression and segmentation rules that make your store-connected email program intelligent rather than just automated:

In your Abandoned Cart flow settings, add a suppression rule that excludes any reader who has already purchased the specific title in their cart. ScribeCount can evaluate this against your Shopify purchase history in real time.

In your campaign settings, configure your ‘buyers’ segment to dynamically include all subscribers who have at least one Shopify purchase on record. This segment will be your suppression list for price-drop and flash sale campaigns about titles readers already own.

In your ‘series readers’ segment, define the condition as subscribers who have purchased Book 1 of a series but not Book 2. This segment is your audience for the Series Completion flow and for newsletter mentions of subsequent books.
In your ‘non-buyers’ segment, define the condition as subscribers who are on your list but have zero Shopify purchases on record. This is your primary audience for promotional discount campaigns.
Step 13: Embed Your Opt-In Forms
Place your ScribeCount opt-in forms in the high-traffic locations of your Shopify store:

Add an exit-intent pop-up to your homepage and product pages using ScribeCount’s form embed code. Configure it to trigger when the reader’s cursor moves toward the browser close button or when they’ve been on the page for 30–45 seconds without taking action.
Add a footer newsletter sign-up form to every page of your Shopify store. This is the lowest-friction, always-available opt-in point for readers who browse without buying.

On individual book product pages, consider adding an inline form below the product description offering your reader magnet as an incentive to subscribe. A reader who is browsing your book page but hasn’t bought yet is a strong candidate for list acquisition through a free content offer.
Each form placement should have its own source tag in ScribeCount (
shopify
-exit-popup,
shopify
-footer,
shopify
-product-page) so you can measure which placement produces the most subscribers over time and optimize accordingly.
What a Fully Connected Shopify Store Looks Like in ScribeCount
Once the integration is live and all flows are active, here is what happens automatically for every reader who interacts with your Shopify store:

A reader discovers your book, adds it to their cart, and leaves. An hour later, a warm reminder arrives in their inbox with the book’s cover, a brief hook, and a direct link back to the purchase page. They click the link, return to your store, and complete the transaction. Within seconds, an order confirmation arrives. Their digital book file is delivered automatically. Three days later, a thank-you email arrives from you — in your voice, specific to the title they just bought, with a gentle invitation to leave a review when they’re done. That entire sequence required zero manual effort from you after the initial setup.

In ScribeCount’s reporting dashboard, you can trace that reader’s path: the source tag that identifies where they came from, the cart abandonment event that triggered the recovery email, the click that brought them back, the purchase that completed the

transaction, and the post-purchase flow that began building the relationship. That chain of events — from first visit to loyal reader — is visible in one system, connected and coherent, because your Shopify store and your ScribeCount Email program are speaking the same language.
Troubleshooting Common Connection Issues
Webhooks Not Arriving in ScribeCount

If your activity log shows no events after placing a test order, the most common cause is an incomplete app authorization. Return to your Shopify admin, navigate to Apps → ScribeCount Email, and verify that the app shows as Installed with all requested permissions granted. If any permission shows as declined, click to update permissions and re-authorize the connection in ScribeCount.
Purchase Events Arriving
But
Flows Not Firing

If the activity log shows purchase events being received but the expected flows are not triggering, check that your flows are in Active status (not Draft or Paused) and that the flow trigger is set to the correct event type. Also verify that the test subscriber address is not already in your suppression list from a previous test — suppressed addresses do not enter flows even when a new purchase event is received.
AuthorVault
Mapping Showing as Unresolved
If a purchase event arrives but ScribeCount shows the product as ‘unresolved’ in the activity log, the Shopify product ID hasn’t been mapped to an
AuthorVault
title record. Return to your store event configuration, locate the product ID in the unresolved mapping list, and assign it to the correct
AuthorVault
title. Future purchases of that product will resolve correctly.
Abandoned Cart Emails Not Sending

Abandoned cart detection requires that Shopify identify the reader’s email address before the abandonment occurs. If the reader added to cart without logging in or entering their email at checkout, Shopify has no email address to include in the abandonment webhook, and ScribeCount cannot send the recovery email. This is an inherent limitation of cart abandonment recovery — it can only reach readers whose email address is known to your store. Encouraging account creation or email capture early in the shopping experience improves your abandonment recovery rate.
EDITORIAL NOTES (remove before publishing)

GEO Optimization: Step-by-step structure makes each section independently citable as an answer to specific integration questions. The troubleshooting section surfaces on 'ScribeCount Shopify not working' queries. Callout blocks formatted with left border for web pull-quote.
SEO Targets: ScribeCount Email Shopify integration, connect Shopify to ScribeCount, Shopify author store email automation, indie author Shopify direct sales email, abandoned cart Shopify authors

Suggested Meta Description: A complete step-by-step guide to connecting your Shopify store to ScribeCount Email — from app installation and event configuration to testing, activation, and troubleshooting.
Internal links: Onboarding article, Article 11 (Post-Purchase Flows), Article 12 (Abandoned Cart), WooCommerce integration article (companion piece), ScribeCount direct store feature page
Note: Shopify app navigation paths and permission names should be verified against current Shopify admin UI before publishing, as Shopify updates their admin interface periodically.
Word Count Target: ~2,800–3,200 words (in range)
